questions.text: Which of the following fundamental data structures operates on a strict Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) basis?
In relational database design, what is the primary purpose of a Foreign Key?
options: To uniquely identify each row in its own table
To reference the primary key of another table
To speed up queries via indexing

questions.text: In network communications, which Transport Layer protocol guarantees reliable delivery of packets and ensures they are received in the correct order?
In Python, what is the output of the expression [1, 2, 3] * 2?
Which type of computer memory is volatile, meaning it requires constant electrical power to maintain its state and loses its data when turned off?
In SQL database queries, which clause is used to filter aggregated group results after a GROUP BY clause has been applied?
Identify this logo:
Identify this logo:
Identify this logo:
Identify this logo:
Identify this logo:
Identify this logo:

Which technology fits these clues?
- Developed by Google in 2014.
- Automates container deployment and scaling.
- Uses concepts like Pods, Nodes, and Services.

Which technology fits these clues?
- Released in 2009 as a NoSQL database.
- Stores data in flexible, JSON-like documents (BSON).
- Avoids schemas and traditional tables.

Which technology fits these clues?
- Created by Guido van Rossum in 1991.
- Uses clean indentation for code block readability.
- The dominant language for Machine Learning and AI.

Which technology fits these clues?
- Developed by Mozilla starting in 2010.
- Guarantees memory safety without a garbage collector.
- Uses an "ownership and borrowing" model.

Which technology fits these clues?
- Launched in 2006 by Amazon.
- The pioneer and market leader in cloud infrastructure.
- Features core services like EC2, S3, and RDS.

Which technology fits these clues?
- Created by Linus Torvalds in 1991.
- Open-source operating system kernel.
- Powers 100% of the world's top 500 supercomputers.
Which AI model family, developed by Google, replaced Bard and is known for its native multimodal capabilities and massive context window?
In the field of Artificial Intelligence, what does the widely used acronym LLM stand for?
Which chipmaker is currently the dominant manufacturer of the high-performance H100 and A100 GPUs used to train state-of-the-art AI systems?
What is the name of OpenAI's text-to-video generative model, announced in 2024, capable of creating highly detailed, realistic video clips from text prompts?
What is the name of the popular AI-powered developer tool launched by GitHub that integrates directly into code editors to suggest code lines and entire functions in real-time?
Which open-source Python library, developed by Meta's AI Research lab, is highly popular for training deep learning neural networks?
An element has width: 100px, padding: 20px, and a 5px border. If its CSS is set to box-sizing: border-box, what is the actual total width rendered on the screen?
Both HTML tags <b> and <strong> make text look identical (bold). What is the main difference between using them?
options: <strong> is modern and uses less RAM to render.
<strong> adds semantic importance for screen readers, while <b> only changes style.
<b> is deprecated and no longer supported in HTML5.
<b> is for uppercase, while <strong> is for lowercase.
It throws a TypeError (reassignment to constant).
It creates a new variable in memory.

questions.text: When you position an element as position: absolute in CSS, it positions itself relative to which ancestor?
options: The nearest ancestor that has a position other than static.

questions.text: In JavaScript, what is the boolean outcome of the expression: 5 == "5"?
When browsing, what does the "S" stand for in the secure web address prefix HTTPS?
Which programming language, developed by Apple, is the modern standard for building iOS and macOS apps (succeeding Objective-C)?
In database management, what is the acronym for the four key properties (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) that guarantee transaction reliability?
What is the acronym for the lightweight, human-readable data format that represents structured objects using curly braces {} and arrays using []?
In Node.js development, what is the three-letter command-line tool used to install, update, and manage third-party project packages?
In HTML, what is the tag name used to define the most important heading of a webpage?
Which CSS box-model property controls the space inside an element, between the content itself and its border?
When a Windows computer suffers a critical system crash, it displays a full-screen error message. What is this screen famously called?
In software and games, what is the term for a hidden feature, message, or joke left by developers for players to find?
What is the name of the classic, pixelated offline game that automatically appears in Google Chrome when your internet connection is lost?
We connect to Wi-Fi daily. What does the acronym "Wi-Fi" officially stand for in technical terms?
options: Nothing (it is just a catchy brand name with no technical meaning)

questions.text: On Windows, what is the legendary three-key shortcut used to quickly open the Task Manager or unlock the computer?
What is the name of the small files stored by web browsers to remember your login sessions and cart items?
Connect the Pictures: Combine the two visual points in the image to identify the network security system.
Connect the Pictures: Combine the two visual points in the image to find the popular web programming language.
Connect the Pictures: Combine the two visual points in the image to find the hardware input device.
Connect the Pictures: Combine the two visual points in the image to identify the short-range wireless standard.
Connect the Pictures: Combine the two visual points in the image to identify the main computer circuit board.
Connect the Pictures: Combine the two visual points in the image to identify the hardware processor component.
